Sports.title: Who is in Tiger Woods' fiercely protective inner circle? Close confidants revealed after golf legend is bailed out of jail again
Sports.content:
Who is in Tiger Woods' fiercely protective inner circle? Close confidants revealed after golf legend is bailed out of jail again

As Tiger Woods was chauffeured away from a Florida jail on Friday night, the golf world was left reeling at scenes all too reminiscent of past scandal.

Hours earlier, Woods was charged with DUI, property damage and refusing to provide a urine sample to police after a two-car rollover crash near his home in Jupiter, Florida.

While Woods appeared to be unharmed as he left the Martin County Jail, the same old questions about the golf legend's immediate future began to echo once again.

It was eerily reminiscent of the panic and concern of five years ago, when Woods faced a similar car crash in California, which shattered his right leg after he was trapped under the vehicle.

Then, Woods leaned on his closest confidants as he endured multiple surgeries and a long road back to recovery. And now, just like then, he will be forced to do the same.

One man who will no doubt be called to Woods' aid once again is Rob McNamara, who has been a fixture by the 15-time major winner's side since his amateur days in southern California.

Tiger Woods is driven away from Martin County Jail after being bonded out on Friday night

Woods stares down the lens in his police mugshot after being arrested for DUI on Friday

Woods stands alongside his Range Rover SUV after clambering out of the passenger side

Now an executive vice president at TGR, Woods' golf-course design firm, McNamara is the son of an Irish father and a 1997 graduate of Santa Clara, where he played on the golf team.

He later had a stint at a start-up golf website, which went bust, but later got a job at IMG, which represented golfers like Arnold Palmer, Annika Sorenstam and Woods, whom McNamara already knew from his days as an amateur golfer in California.

At first, McNamara's job was to protect Woods' rights, according to a 2023 Golfweek article, but he soon made himself irreplaceable in the golfer's life. So when Woods launched his own course design firm, TGR, he took McNamara with him.

But McNamara is more than just a long-time business partner. He also serves as a second set of eyes for his long-time friend, who has been without a coach for nearly a decade.

'I mean, he knows my game,' Woods told Golf TV in 2019. 'One, he's a good player. Two, he's got a good eye and he has seen me go through changes and iterations in the game of golf, but at the end of the day I try and use Rob for what I'm trying to work on now, because right now it's different.'

At the time, Woods was recovering from a back surgery to alleviate pain in his back and leg.

'I'm limited what I can do,' Woods continued, 'and not only am I limited in what I can do, I'm limited in how much I can practice.'

It's something that McNamara intuitively understood, perhaps, better than any coach could.

Rob McNamara has been a fixture by the 15-time major winner's side since his amateur days

McNamara was seen hugging the victorious golfer on the 18th green in Augusta in 2019

'Because I've played with him so much and practiced with him so much over the years I kind of know what it should look like or what typically he's trying to do,' McNamara said. 'And a lot of it this last year is Tiger is typically telling me, 'Hey, I'm feeling this. Will you just watch and make sure I'm not doing this or I am doing this?'

'Then all I'm doing is kind of quickly confirming it for him, yes, and then kind of backing away.'

In 2019, when Woods turned the clock back to win the Masters after years of injury woes, it was McNamara who was seen hugging the victorious golfer on the 18th green in Augusta.

Also pictured in the iconic scene on the edge of the 18th green at Augusta National, were Woods's dearest loved ones; his mom and his children.

Woods' mother, Kultida, had been a 'rock' in her son's life, standing by him through scandal and, of course, his previous 2021 accident.

He was extremely close to his mom, who saw him emerge to win the Masters as a 21-year-old in 1997, conquer the game in an era of relentless dominance before standing by him during his downfall from 2009's sex scandal that cost him his first marriage and millions in endorsement deals.

However, Woods sadly lost 'Tida' at the age of 80 in early 2025. He described her as his 'biggest fan and greatest supporter' in a heartbreaking statement at the time.

'It's been a tough process to go through,' Woods said in his first interview since her death in February 2025. 'But I've had amazing family support, all the players have reached out that were playing here this week.'

Woods is pictured with his two children, daughter Sam, 18, and son Charlie, 17

Woods lost his 'rock,' mother Kultida, in early 2025 at the age of 80. Pictured in 2008

That 'amazing' family includes his two children - daughter Sam, 18, and son, Charlie, 17 - whom he shares with his ex-wife, Elin Nordegren.

Woods first met Swedish model Nordegren in 2001, before proposing two years later. The couple then made it official at a lavish wedding in 2004.

Nordegren divorced Tiger in 2010 after six years of marriage when she discovered the golfing great was allegedly cheating on her during Thanksgiving week with a woman named Rachel Uchitel.

Yet, despite the ugly split, the former couple appear to have repaired their relationship for the sake of their children. Woods and Nordegren are frequently pictured alongside each other as they support their children in their various endeavors.

Most recently, they were pictured together to celebrate Charlie's latest golfing achievement.

Charlie, who recently committed to Florida State University, and his Benjamin School boys golf teammates were honored in a ceremony to celebrate their latest state championship.

'Having the relationship that I have now with her is fantastic,' Woods told Time in 2015. 'We both know that the most important things in our lives are our kids. I wish I would have known that back then.'

Woods is now in a relationship with Vanessa Trump, whose eldest daughter Kai is also a student at the Benjamin School and a promising golfer.

Woods was married to Swedish model Elin Nordegren from 2004 to 2010 and had two kids

The golf star is now in a relationship with Vanessa Trump, the ex-wife of Donald Trump Jr

Woods' agent, Mark Steinberg, has been by his side since the early years of his career

Woods has been dating Vanessa for over a year - their relationship was first reported by the Daily Mail in March 2025 after they started seeing each other around Thanksgiving 2024.

Vanessa, the ex-wife of Donald Trump Jr., has been supporting her beau at several golfing events. Most recently, the couple were captured putting on a loved-up public display of affection at the finals of TGL on Tuesday.

The finals, which saw Woods' Jupiter Links fall short to Los Angeles Golf Club, marked the beginning of his comeback fueling anticipation that he could be making a return to The Masters. A return that, following the events of Friday night, now seems a million miles away once again.

But it would not be the first comeback Woods has battled through, and again he would have the support of one of his most trusted advisors.

His agent, Mark Steinberg, has been by his side since the early years of his career. The golfing icon retained Steinberg as his agent in 1998 after he fired Hughes Norton, who described Woods as 'zombie-like.'

When Steinberg made the move from IMG to Excel Sports Management, where he is a partner and heads the golf division, Woods followed suit.

The agent has been by Woods' side ever since, saying of their relationship in 2016: 'We are unwavering in our commitment to each other.'

Steinberg, attended the University of Illinois and made the basketball team as a walk-on, boasts a client list that also features the likes of Woods' close on-course friend, Justin Thomas.

Notah Begay III (right) has been friends with Woods since their junior golf days

Fellow professional Justin Thomas has become a close friend to the Woods family

Five-time major winner Rory McIlroy is a friend and business partner of the legend

Thomas is among the golfers closest to Woods on the PGA Tour, alongside Rory McIlroy.

McIlroy is not only a friend to Woods but also a business partner. The pair teamed up to launch their TMRW Sports venture, which created TGL, their tech-infused, indoor golf league.

While McIlroy and Thomas only entered Woods' circle as young whippersnappers well into the midst of his decorated career, there is one player who has played alongside the legend since the beginning of his career.

Notah Begay III has been friends with Woods since their junior golf days, meeting when Begay was nine and Woods even younger.

They solidified their friendship as teammates at Stanford, before both making it to the PGA Tour.

'Looking back on it that wasn't something that we focused on but we were probably two of the only minorities playing at that level so that was something we connected with as well,' Begay said of their junior days in a 2022 interview.

'It's really truly been one of the most significant friendships in my life.'

Begay has been a steadfast supporter of Woods throughout his turbulent public life, even going as far as to act as a mouthpiece for his friend, feeding information about his various recoveries to the public.



Now, it appears Woods' oldest friend will be called upon once again.

Sports.title: Leicester Tigers boss Geoff Parling involved in X-rated exchange with TNT Sport presenters over pre-match incident
Sports.content:
Leicester Tigers boss Geoff Parling involved in X-rated exchange with TNT Sport presenters over pre-match incident

Leicester Tigers boss Geoff Parling had an X-rated exchange with TNT Sport presenters ahead of their Prem Rugby clash with Gloucester.

Footage showed Leicester players warming up ahead of the match at Villa Park, where Gloucester had opted to stage a showpiece match.

Out on the touchline presenter Craig Doyle invited Liam MacDevitt to attempt a conversion at the posts, joking it was part of an initiation due to being a new part of their team.

Doyle said: 'You're going to kick this ball', with former footballer-turned presenter MacDevitt quipping 'this is going to be a debut defining moment' as he prepared to take the kick.

As MacDevitt ran up and struck the ball towards the posts and close to the Leicester players, Parling and a member of his coaching staff could be seen approaching the TNT presenters.

Doyle first congratulated him on a 'good effort' and jokingly asked 'would you sign him, Geoff?' as the Leicester boss approached.

WARNING: STRONG LANGUAGE

This lads first day on TNT rugby…



Big Geoff Parling wasn’t impressed…



Valuable lesson learnt;

Don’t mess with the warm up… pic.twitter.com/89sFyaCBDa — Enge Gray (@Enge21) March 28, 2026

Presenter Liam MacDevitt attempted a conversion as part of a light-hearted initiation to the TNT Sports team, but it drew the attention of Leicester Tigers boss Geoff Parling

Parling was seen approaching presenters Craig Doyle and MacDevitt, visibly unhappy after the ball had been kicked towards his players, who were warming up

Parling was seen shoving Doyle and declared 'It's f***ing not on' as he told the presenters to get off the pitch

Parling, however, was less than impressed with the Leicester coach telling the presenters 'That’s not on, that’s f***ing not on. Get off the pitch.'

The Leicester coach was seen shoving Doyle, who then offered an apology.

'Sorry, Geoff not happy about that,' Doyle said.

'Apologies for the language, Geoff understandably upset about that, let's just step off the pitch here for a second.'

Viewers of the coverage on TNT, who pay around £40m to show Prem Rugby each season, were split over the incident.

Some argued that Parling had overreacted to the incident, while others were critical of the TNT presenters for interrupting Leicester Tigers' warm-up ahead of the match.

Leicester Tigers would ultimately earn a 36-17 victory over Gloucester in the early kick-off, lifting Parling's side up to third in the table.

Leicester won the Slater Cup as a result, with the fixture between the teams played in honour of their former player Ed Slater, who is suffering from motor neurone disease.

The match at Villa Park has helped to raise money for the charity founded in his name, 4Ed.



Prior to the match, Lewis Moody - who is also suffering from MND - completed a charity bike ride with his 2003 World Cup winning captain Martin Johnson.

Sports.title: Defiant Senegal stars PARADE the AFCON trophy - days after being controversially stripped of their crown
Sports.content:
Defiant Senegal stars PARADE the AFCON trophy - days after being controversially stripped of their crown

Senegal continued to protest the stripping of their Africa Cup of Nations title as they paraded the trophy around the Stade de France before their latest international match.

The Confederation of African Football appeal board took the unprecedented action to essentially disqualify Senegal earlier this month after their players walked off the field in protest following a decision to hand their hosts a penalty in the decider on January 18.

Amid tumultuous scenes, the penalty was subsequently missed when Senegal returned to the field and they went on to win 1-0. In an unprecedented ruling 58 days later, Morocco have now been handed the game 3-0 after an appeal, and the title with it.

A spokesperson for CAF said their appeal board decided that 'the Senegal National Team is declared to have forfeited the Final'.

They added that an appeal from Morocco was 'admissible and upheld' after Senegal's behaviour 'infringed Article 82' of its regulations.

But Senegal, having appealed the decision, continue to insist they are champions, and, ahead of their friendly match with Peru in front of 70,000 fans in France on Sunday, showed off the trophy.

Senegal have paraded their Africa Cup of Nations trophy despite being stripped of the title

They took to the field to lift the title despite a ruling claiming they forfeited the final 3-0

The likes of Edouard Mendy and Iliman Ndiaye were in attendance as the Senegal squad took the trophy onto the field, and they held it high in the stands too as their fans cheered.

Everton's Idrissa Gueye is among the players who have protested the ruling.

He said: 'I think this decision is just ridiculous. We won the game on the pitch not in offices and as a Senegalese player I'm just proud of what we did.

'We deserve this trophy. We fight on the pitch and everyone knows what happened and I think if you play 10 times this game we would win it 10 times.'

Senegal have lodged an appeal of their own, with the Court of Arbitration for Sport (Cas) saying it will decide on the appeal 'as swiftly as possible.'

Cas director general Matthieu Reeb added: 'We understand that teams and fans are eager to know the final decision, and we will ensure that arbitration proceedings are conducted as swiftly as possible, while respecting the right of all parties to a fair hearing.'

In the 98th minute of the game in Rabat at the start of the year, Senegal's players made their exit in protest at an earlier goal and the awarding of the later penalty. They were off for around 17 minutes as chaos unfolded with Senegal fans attempting to invade the pitch.

Senegal eventually returned after being persuaded by former Liverpool forward Sadio Mane to come back on.

The likes of Edouard Mendy and Iliman Ndiaye were in attendance at the Stade de France

Players from Senegal and Morocco clashed after a penalty award before eventual champions Senegal left the field in the final

Real Madrid's Brahim Diaz took the penalty which was saved by former Chelsea stopper Edouard Mendy and the game went into extra-time - where Senegal triumphed thanks to a goal from Pape Gueye to give them what they thought was their second AFCON title in five years.



However, Morocco appealed and in a stunning decision CAF have taken the extraordinary step.

Sports.title: Tiger Woods should NEVER play golf again, top analyst claims after latest car crash and DUI arrest: 'Keeps hurting himself'
Sports.content:
Tiger Woods has been urged to never play golf again and stop putting 'stress' on his body following his shock car crash and DUI arrest in Florida.

The golf legend, 50, was arrested on Friday after he tried to overtake a truck and trailer at high speed on a residential Jupiter Island road, clipping the back of the vehicle and tipping his own SUV onto its side.

After clambering out of the passenger side window of his vehicle, he refused to give a urine sample to cops and was arrested for DUI, property damage and refusal to submit to a lawful test. He was transported to Martin County Public Safety Complex where he was booked at around 3pm and had his latest mugshot taken, before being released at around 11pm.

Woods was involved in a similar crash in 2021 in California, which shattered his right leg after he was trapped under the vehicle. He needed multiple surgeries in order to recover from his injuries.

He was also previously pictured in a police mugshot in 2017 after being arrested for his first DUI, when police found him slumped in his car in Florida with five drugs in his system, including two painkillers.

And following his latest arrest, which comes just days after he made his hugely-anticipated return to golf at an event on Tuesday night, longtime pundit and analyst Brandel Chamblee believes Woods should never think about competing again.

Tiger Woods has been urged to never play golf again following his latest crash and DUI arrest

Woods stares down the lens in his police mugshot after being arrested for DUI on Friday

'Well, why would he need to play golf anymore? You know, I think he should probably ask himself that. Consider not playing golf anymore,' Chamblee said on The Golf Channel after Woods' arrest on Friday.

'It’s clear that Tiger Woods has a history of pushing himself to his physical limits beyond his physical limits. The stories are of him not needing a lot of sleep. When you sleep, your body repairs itself.

'He’s up two, three, four in the morning at the gym, grinding it out all day long. You know this puts a considerable stress on what is already a fractured and fragile body. And to the degree that he keeps hurting himself, injuring himself and requiring surgeries.'

Woods, who is currently dating Vanessa Trump, has not given up hope of returning to competitive golf despite the serious injuries he has suffered over the years.

Hours before his crash was first reported, President Donald Trump revealed he will not be playing in this year's Masters amid speculation over a shock comeback.

Chamblee added about the 15-time major winner: 'He’s done his work in the game of golf. Nothing he’s going to do on the Champions Tour, with all due respect, to senior golf, we all love it, but it will add nothing really to his legacy. And you know, it’s clear that since 2021 when he’s come back, he can’t play at a competitive level on the PGA Tour.

'His body just won’t let him do what his talents have previously let him do. That seems like the only circumstance that would alleviate this problem, beyond that you just hope that he gets the care and the help that he needs.'

Cops released Woods' remarkable mugshot to the public shortly after 10pm, with his eyes puffy and bloodshot in the image. An hour later, he was bonded out and driven away from the jail, as fans and reporters swarmed the car for a glimpse of him.

Woods was driven away from Martin County Jail after being bonded out on Friday night

Top golf analyst Brandel Chamblee believes he should never compete in the sport again

Chamblee urged Woods to walk away from golf to avoid putting his body through 'stress'

Woods was supported by girlfriend Vanessa Trump on his latest return to action at TGL

In photos taken by a Daily Mail photographer, he can be seen looking stony-faced with his lips pursed, before momentarily glancing out of the passenger side window at the gathered masses before disappearing into the night.

At the scene of the accident, Woods passed a breathalyzer test after showing 'triple zeros', but then refused to give a urine sample twice, once on the roadside and then again at the jail.

'He is cooperative, but he was not trying to incriminate himself, so he was careful in what he said and didn't say,' Sheriff Budensiek explained. 'When it came time again for the test, the urine test at the jail, he stopped that.

'On scene, we had [Drug Recognition] experts evaluating him and they believed from on scene that he was not impaired with alcohol, but they believe it was some type of medication or drug. And again, at the jail he cooperated with the breathalyzer, and then the urine [test] he wanted no part in.

'He has a right to refuse that test. There is a statute that he will be charged with for refusing to take that test, but we will never get definitive results as to what he was impaired on at the time of the crash.'

Woods has been dating Vanessa - the ex-wife of Trump's son Donald Jr - for over a year. Their relationship was first reported by the Daily Mail in March 2025 after they started seeing each other around Thanksgiving 2024.

Vanessa, who watched on as Tiger made his golf return at TGL on Tuesday, filed for an uncontested divorce from Donald Jr in March 2018.

Sports.title: Tiger Woods is lucky he's never killed someone with his reckless driving... golf has indulged his shameful behaviour for far too long - the sport should be helping its fallen hero instead, writes OLIVER HOLT
Sports.content:
Tiger Woods is lucky he's never killed someone with his reckless driving... golf has indulged his shameful behaviour for far too long - the sport should be helping its fallen hero instead, writes OLIVER HOLT

Golf has clung to the shell of the man that was once Tiger Woods for too long as it struggles to chart a future without the player who has been the greatest draw, and money-machine, in the modern game.

It has indulged his recklessness for too long and looked the other way when he has risked his own life and the lives of others with a terrifying level of negligence behind the wheel of a series of cars.

It has watched on for too long as he failed to express penitence or regret for his various vehicular misadventures. It has enabled him by treating him like a hero for misdeeds that do not befit a hero.

Golf has let him down for too long, too – its focus has been on encouraging him to get back to the course and mend his injuries as soon as possible when it should have been on helping him to overcome a far bigger battle, an apparent dependency on painkilling drugs.

The truth is this: Woods is lucky – and golf is lucky – that he has not killed an innocent person in the series of accidents and incidents in which he has been involved in the last nine years.

In May, 2017, not long after he underwent spinal fusion surgery, Woods was found passed out behind the wheel of a Mercedes-Benz on the side of the road around 3am in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida.

Tiger Woods stares down the lens in his police mugshot after being arrest for DUI on Friday afternoon

Woods flipped his SUV onto its side after clipping a trailer (right) while attempting to overtake

He was found to have prescription drugs in his system; Vicodin, Dilaudid, Xanax, Ambien and THC. He entered an in-patient rehab facility for prescription medication addiction. He later pleaded guilty to reckless driving.

In February, 2021, on his way to a photo-shoot near Los Angeles, Woods lost control of his car at speed. It struck the central reservation, crossed into the opposite lane, rolled over several times and then plunged off the road and down an embankment.

Police said he was driving at 87mph in a 45mph zone. There was an empty, unlabeled pill container in the car. Woods told police he thought he was in Florida. They did not run a toxicology test.

Woods was seriously injured and required multiple operations on fractures in his lower right leg but when he eventually returned to golf, none of the questions he was asked concerned whether he felt any contrition for the harm he could have caused others.

The only thought appeared to be for Woods’ physical recovery and for when and where he might return to golf, not for the fact that he had quite clearly become a danger to others and to himself.

And now it has happened again. Now we are being assailed with more blurry-eyed images of Woods staring hazily into a police camera for another mugshot after rolling his Land Rover on a quiet residential street near his home in Jupiter, Florida.

Tiger Woods was driven away from Martin County Jail after being bonded out on Friday night

Woods did not have alcohol in his system but he refused to take a urine test and police at the scene said he appeared to be impaired. We have seen this movie before and it’s starting to feel like a tragedy.

The pattern repeats. It has been repeating since 2009 when Woods drove his car into a fire hydrant while attempting to flee his wife Elin Nordegren, who was said to have confronted him in the midst of problems in their marriage.

If Woods appears to have a dependency on pain-killing drugs, he also has a dependency on golf and golf has a dependency on him. In the past, when he has transgressed, golf has forgiven him and then golf has redeemed him.

The outstanding example of that pattern was his recovery from the spinal fusion surgery and his rehabilitation after he was found passed out in his Mercedes, which had two flat tires, in 2017.

Woods has had a series of back operations in the long span of his incredible career and even though there were times in the build-up to that surgery when he could barely walk, let alone play golf, he pulled off one of the greatest comeback triumphs in the history of sport when he won the Masters in 2019, his 15th major.

And the narrative, of course, is that that makes all the pain and all the suffering and all the pills and all the car crashes worthwhile.

The crash is the another low point for the legendary golfer, coming weeks before the Masters

Even last week, Woods was still talking about the prospect of returning to the Masters next month for another appearance at Augusta National, the course that he loves more than any other, the course where the full bloom of his incredible talent was first advertised to the world when he won the tournament in 1997.

Woods played in an indoor event staged by The Golf League in Palm Beach, that was attended by his girlfriend, Vanessa Trump, and assorted members of the glitterati, and refused to rule out playing in Georgia.

That dream felt unlikely last week. It feels impossible now after Woods latest misadventure.

It is time for Woods to stop trying to prove he can be competitive again. It is time for golf to let him go.



It is time he learned to help himself before it is too late. The picture of that flipped car on the road felt like a metaphor for a gilded life that is turning into an American tragedy.

Sports.title: There's one missing ingredient from this England squad - and no easy solutions for Thomas Tuchel as he faces decision that could make or break World Cup hopes, writes JACK GAUGHAN
Sports.content:
There will not be that much debate over who starts in Dallas. Maybe there is a debate to be had around the left winger and to a lesser extent, full back, but broadly speaking, it is picking itself.

Thomas Tuchel can probably name eight with absolute certainty. Many of them sat up in a box at Wembley on Friday, Harry Kane later striding out into the night with the aura of a striker who pleads with you not to worry, he will immediately fix the attacking ills at play during the draw with Uruguay.

Barring injury, it is Kane, it is Declan Rice, it is Elliot Anderson, it is Bukayo Saka, it is Marc Guehi. John Stones, Jordan Pickford, Jude Bellingham. Probably Nico O’Reilly and definitely Reece James if he proves his fitness.

None of that is the issue for Tuchel. The issue for Tuchel is in the searing heat of the United States – and more notably, a potential last-16 tie with Mexico at the Azteca and its altitude – who is changing the course of games from the bench?

With one friendly to go until the World Cup squad announcement, nobody is quite sure. Phil Foden and Cole Palmer have 20 goals between them this season from 66 games. Pitted against each other for the third No 10 spot, both appear short on confidence.

Tuchel has used the stick with Palmer in recent days, the carrot with Foden. Palmer’s influence on the Uruguay game, particularly his set piece deliveries, gave him an edge but there were moments when his natural instinct was similar to Foden – a burst past a marker before heading backwards when the pitch seemed to open up.

England have little that can help them off the bench right now - Phil Foden (left) and Cole Palmer (right) are both short on confidence

These are tricky moods for an international manager to fix in such a short period of time. He has talked about how all of these players are big stars for their clubs and how they need to lean into disappointment at England selections for the betterment of the squad.

Effectively to leave the ego at the door. But Tuchel’s job here is bordering on the opposite, to lift them up from scatty club campaigns. Foden has not started six of the last eight Premier League games for Manchester City and while Tuchel has praised his attitude in training, especially during Thursday’s defensive drills, to snap out of that malaise is no mean feat.

Whether he has enough time – especially given the ankle injury suffered at the studs of Uruguay’s Ronald Araujo – is uncertain. The same can be said for Noni Madueke, whose year has yielded only seven goals and who left Wembley in a knee brace.

The one man who truly benefited from Friday was Eberechi Eze, with a spot for Morgan Rogers also seemingly under no threat either.

Something needs to click with a few of them, Foden a prime example when drifting in from the right and refusing to let fly with that angular strike into the far corner that we’ve seen so many times and on the biggest stages. Tuchel knows that is there somewhere, somewhere deep inside, and is giving him every chance to locate it.

Time is running out though and England are not going to North America with the assurance that an Ollie Watkins lies in wait ready to turn a semi-final on its head from the bench. Dominic Calvert-Lewin and Dominic Solanke did much to further their cause, the latter potentially glancing wide a spot on the place from Palmer’s wicked cross.

That throws up two prospects: either bringing Watkins (similarly to Foden and Palmer, on 10 goals for the year) in from the cold despite patchy form or persevere with Foden as a false nine, a role he has previously starred in for Pep Guardiola. That is how Kane plays so may represent the most logical like-for-like swap.

There is nobody banging down the door to reveal themselves as this summer’s Watkins and Tuchel is going to have to gamble on a couple of them – unless somebody barges through against Japan on Tuesday. It is the biggest call of his tenure, because whoever is chosen to act as those fresh legs in the stifling conditions once the knockouts hit will have England’s fate at their feet.

Sports.title: Tiger Woods is RELEASED from jail after eight hours behind bars... as bleary-eyed mugshot from DUI arrest emerges
Sports.content:
Tiger Woods has been released from jail after spending eight hours behind bars in the wake of his terrifying high-speed crash and subsequent DUI arrest.

The golf legend, 50, was arrested on Friday afternoon after he tried to overtake a truck and trailer at high speed on a residential Jupiter Island road, clipping the back of the vehicle and tipping his own SUV onto its side.

After clambering out of the passenger side window of his vehicle, Woods refused to give a urine sample to cops and was arrested for DUI, property damage and refusal to submit to a lawful test.

He was transported to Martin County Public Safety Complex where he was booked at around 3pm, and his latest mugshot taken.

Cops released the remarkable image to the public shortly after 10pm, with Woods' eyes puffy and bloodshot. An hour later, he was bonded out and driven away from the jail, as fans and reporters swarmed the car for a glimpse of him.

In photos taken by a Daily Mail photographer, Woods can be seen looking stony-faced with his lips pursed, before momentarily glancing out of the passenger side window at the gathered masses before disappearing into the night.

Tiger Woods is driven away from Martin County Jail after being bonded out on Friday night

Woods appeared stony-faced as he sat in the passenger seat after eight hours behind bars

Woods stares down the lens in his police mugshot after being arrest for DUI on Friday afternoon

Woods pursed his lips and glanced out of the window at reporters as he left the jail

Woods stands alongside his Range Rover SUV after clambering out of the passenger side

It was earlier revealed by Sheriff John Budensiek that the 15-time major champion would be required to spend at least eight hours in prison before being allowed to leave on bail.

Speaking to reporters, the sheriff said of Woods' jail time: 'It doesn't matter who you are, if you break the law we're gonna follow the law. That's a really easy path to take.

'Now as far as being in the jail, we're gonna make sure he's safe. We're not gonna put him in general population, he's not gonna be with other inmates that can try and hurt him or try to capitalize on what he did.

'He'll pay the price, but he's not gonna pay the price by getting punished in jail. He was not injured. He was lethargic on scene, but we believe that was because of what he was intoxicated on.'

At the scene of the accident, Woods passed a breathalyzer test after showing 'triple zeros', but then refused to give a urine sample twice, once on the roadside and then again at the jail.

'He is cooperative, but he was not trying to incriminate himself, so he was careful in what he said and didn't say,' Sheriff Budensiek explained. 'When it came time again for the test, the urine test at the jail, he stopped that.

Join the debate Does fame lead to leniency - or are stars held to the same standard as everyone else?

Fans and reporters crowded the black SUV as it left the Martin County Jail shortly after 11pm

Woods flipped his SUV onto its side after clipping a trailer (right) while attempting to overtake

Woods is seen in a police booking photo after his arrest on suspicion of DUI on May 29, 2017

'On scene, we had [Drug Recognition] experts evaluating him and they believed from on scene that he was not impaired with alcohol, but they believe it was some type of medication or drug. And again, at the jail he cooperated with the breathalyzer, and then the urine [test] he wanted no part in.

'He has a right to refuse that test. There is a statute that he will be charged with for refusing to take that test, but we will never get definitive results as to what he was impaired on at the time of the crash.'

Woods, who lives on Jupiter Island around two miles from where the incident occurred, was involved in a similar crash in 2021 in California, which shattered his right leg after he was trapped under the vehicle. He needed multiple surgeries in order to recover from his injuries.

The accident, the third crash he has been involved in in recent years, comes just days after Woods made his hugely anticipated return to golf at an event on Tuesday night, where girlfriend Vanessa Trump was watching him play.

Woods was previously pictured in a police mugshot in 2017 after being arrested for his first DUI, when police found him slumped in his car in Florida with five drugs in his system, including two painkillers.

The golf legend kisses girlfriend Vanessa Trump on the cheek on his return to golf on Tuesday

Woods pictured with his two kids Sam (center) and Charlie (left) in Florida in 2023

He subsequently checked into rehab for painkiller addiction.

He was involved in another high-profile crash in 2009 when he hit a fire hydrant after being chased from his home by then-wife Elin Nordegren, which led him to admit to multiple affairs and drug use.

Woods and Nordegren eventually divorced in 2010 but have remained friends while co-parenting their children.

'Having the relationship that I have now with her is fantastic,' Woods told Time in 2015. 'We both know that the most important things in our lives are our kids. I wish I would have known that back then.'

Their 17-year-old son, Charlie, is an aspiring golfer and the former couple often reunite to support him at events. They also have a daughter, Sam, who is 18.

Woods has been dating Vanessa Trump for over a year - their relationship was first reported by the Daily Mail in March 2025 after they started seeing each other around Thanksgiving 2024.

Vanessa filed for an uncontested divorce from ex-husband Donald Trump Jr. in March 2018.

Sports.title: Connor Beasley stars at Dubai World Cup with 376-1 double - and flies back for Doncaster
Sports.content:
Connor Beasley stars at Dubai World Cup with 376-1 double - and flies back for Doncaster

The jockey partnered Native Approach (28-1) in the Group One Al Quoz Sprint Sponsored By Azizi Developments and Dark Saffron (12-1) in the Dubai Golden Shaheen Sponsored By Nakheel for trainer Ahmad bin Harmash

View 2 Images Connor Beasley (aboard Dark Saffron) had two winners on the night (Image: Cedric Lane/Shutterstock)

Jockey Connor Beasley jetted in for the prestigious Dubai World Cup meeting to ride a 376-1 double for trainer Ahmad bin Harmash.

‌



Beasley, who had ridden at Southwell and Wolverhampton in the last seven days, starred on Native Approach (28-1) in the Group One Al Quoz Sprint Sponsored By Azizi Developments and Dark Saffron (12-1), the Dubai Golden Shaheen (Sponsored By Nakheel) hero.

‌



Both Meydan winners came home ahead of a hot favourite, with Lazzat (5-6f) only third behind the first-named horse and then Bentornato (5-4f) claiming the runner-up spot.

‌



Beasley first went to the United Arab Emirates around eight seasons ago to be second jockey for Ahmad bin Harmash.

Injury stops joint leader Freddie Gordon in title race for conditional jump jockeys READ MORE :

Free shop bets with William Hill every day for the Grand National Festival with your Mirror READ MORE :

And Native Approach, formerly trained by Charlie Appleby in Newmarket and reinvented as a six furlong horse, proved his 50-1 success at the track on February 28 was no fluke as held Lugal by a neck.

“He’s a bundle of speed and when I first sat on him at the start of the season I did say to the boss he feels like a sprinter," Beasley said.

‌



“He puts his heart on his sleeve every morning, he’s not easy. Once the Japanese horse came to me tonight he was very determined."

View 2 Images (Image: AP)

Beasley said the team worked on Dark Saffron, who joined thoroughbreds like Caller One and Mind Your Biscuits as back-to-back winners of the Dubai Golden Shaheen, after he had an incident in the gates.

Article continues below

"He banged his head and sort of lost his way," the rider said. "We took the headgear off and tried him on turf, just experimenting to help him regain that confidence. He’s a top-quality horse, and it’s great to see him back performing at the highest level.

He added: "Ahmad has been been a big supporter and put plenty of trust in me and it just shows when a team clicks it goes through to the horses as well as the people as well.”

Beasley had little time for celebration as he was booked to fly back to Britain for five rides at Doncaster on Sunday, with the shortest-priced of them Backspin, 10-1 with the sponsors for division one of the William Hill Epic Boosts Handicap over seven furlongs.

Sports.title: Moses Itauma fight LIVE results, start time and stream
Sports.content:
Moses Itauma will look to take another step towards a heavyweight world title shot tonight when he takes on Jermaine Franklin.

The next step of Itauma’s rapid rise up the ranks of the blue riband division takes place two months later than planned after the Kent man suffered a torn bicep earlier this year. Itauma tipped the scales at 242.1lbs (17st 4lbs) on Friday – three pounds lighter than for his first-round demolition of Dillian Whyte in August – and Franklin weighed 258.4lbs (18st 6lbs) before a respectful face-off.

Franklin took Whyte and Anthony Joshua the distance earlier in his career and has been drafted in to do the same with Itauma. You can keep up to date with all the results here with the main event due to start at around 11pm streamed live on DAZN...

Moses Itauma vs Jermaine Franklin - TBC

Nathan Heaney vs Gerome Warburton - TBC

Willy Hutchinson vs Ezra Taylor - TBC

Shakiel Thompson vs Brad Pauls - TBC

Liam Davies vs Francesco Grandelli - TBC

Michael Gomez Jr vs Jordan Flynn - TBC

Alex Murphy vs Josh Holmes - TBC

Billy Deniz vs Grant Dennis - TBC

Nelson Birchall vs Ryan Griffiths via ninth-round TKO: One of three fighting brothers, Nelson Birchall was crowned the English featherweight champion with a stoppage win the penultimate round. Birchall, who is now in line to fight for the British title, had Ryan Griffiths pinned against the ropes and referee Howard Foster stepped in to wave the fight off.

Aadam Hamed bt Michael Mooney via UD : After just one fight last year, Aadam Hamed will be looking to make up for lost time in 2026 and comfortably outpointed Michael Mooney over found rounds in front of his famous father. I'm A Celeb star Aitch was also in attendance as Hamed won each of the four rounds to move to 7-0.

John Joe Carrigan bt Danny Costello via third-round KO: A straight-forward win for the talented John Joe Carrigan who picked up his second professional win. This was is first outing since last October and he will be looking to kick on this year. On this occasion, the referee stepped in to save Dave Costello from further punishment in the penultimate round.

Sports.content:

Sports.title: Keely Hodgkinson comes out firing with star close to being denied her ultimate wish
Sports.content:
Keely Hodgkinson comes out firing with star close to being denied her ultimate wish

Keely Hodgkinson has again made her feelings clear about the World Athletics Championship potentially being held in London

View 3 Images Keely Hodgkinson has taken a fresh swipe at West Ham (Image: Getty Images)

Keely Hodgkinson has taken a fresh swipe at West Ham for potentially wrecking her dream of competing at a major championship on home soil. Plans have been drawn up to stage the 2029 World Athletics Championship at the London Stadium but the Hammers, who hold a long-term lease at the venue, have reportedly refused to relocate for the proposed September dates.

‌



The club have previously stated they have an agreement in place granting them priority use of the former Olympic stadium during the football season. Hodgkinson provoked a fierce response from some West Ham supporters earlier this week with a tongue-in-cheek post on X about the situation.

‌



She wrote: "The GB team will bring back more medals to that stadium than west ham have seen in their entire history."

‌



The 24-year-old, who won Olympic 800m gold in Paris in 2024, has since doubled down on her criticism of the Hammers. The Manchester United fan insisted she enjoyed the reaction to her medals comment.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

She posted: "Thoroughly enjoyed the rattled comments under this, but in all seriousness, to have a global championships back in London would be incredible for our sport. I didn't think we'd get the opportunity again during my career, the British crowd would fill it everyday."

‌



Keely Hodginson mocks Premier League club in brutal tweet from Team GB Olympic hero READ MORE :

Keely Hodgkinson delivered on F-bomb demand after defying her own coaches READ MORE :

In a fresh dig, she pointed out the Hammers were tenants, not owners, of the stadium, which they moved into in 2016 after leaving Upton Park.

She added: "Seems silly for London to be taken out of the running, over a football team not compromising on a stadium they pay RENT for when it's only a few extra away games, everything's always all about money and never moments. Let us have this moment!!! Pretty please."

View 3 Images Hodgkinson wasn't bothered by the backlash from Hammers fans (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Hodgkinson, who had to settle for bronze at last year's World Championship in Tokyo after battling back from injury, recently clinched gold by storming to victory in the 800m at the World Indoor Championships in Poland.

She clocked a championship record time of one minute, 55.3 seconds, the second-fastest indoor performance in history behind the world record she set in Lievin, France in February.

Hodgkinson is in the form of her life after rebounding from an injury-riddled 2025. She is overflowing with confidence that another world record is a question of when, not if.

‌



View 3 Images West Ham are reportedly unwilling to vacate the London Stadium for the World Athletics Championship (Image: PA)

The women's outdoor 800m record of 1:53.28, set more than 40 years ago by Jarmila Kratochvilova, is the oldest in track and field. Hodgkinson said: "To have that confidence back in me, I've not missed anything. I was like, 'If you're beating me, you're going to run damn fast to do it'.

"I wanted to go out there and put on a bit of a show. I felt like I had the strength. Three rounds in three days is tough, but I did it!"

Article continues below

Sports.title: Coach slammed after swearing and pushing presenter in awkward moment live on TV
Sports.content:
Coach slammed after swearing and pushing presenter in awkward moment live on TV

TNT Sports presenter Craig Doyle had an awkward confrontation with Geoff Parling after the Leicester Tigers coach was angered by the TV cameras interrupting his side's warm-up

View 2 Images Geoff Parling was not happy with TNT Sports host Craig Doyle (Image: TNT Sports)

Leicester Tigers head coach Geoff Parling clashed with TNT Sports presenter Craig Doyle in a heated encounter live on TV on Saturday. TNT Sports were at Villa Park to broadcast Leicester against Gloucester, only for their build-up to take an unexpected twist.

‌



The Tigers beat Gloucester 36-17 to win the Slater Cup, which is played in honour of ex-player Ed Slater, who is suffering from motor neurone disease. But the broadcast of the Prem fixture did not go to plan.

‌



As part of their build-up, Doyle invited ex-footballer turned presenter Liam McDevitt to kick a penalty as part of an initiation after joining the channel. "This could be a debut-defining moment," McDevitt said before attempting his kick.

‌



The ball landed wide of the posts, close to where Leicester's players were warming up. "It's a good effort," declared Doyle, as Parling walked over with his hands in his pockets shaking his head.

Moses Itauma fight time tonight and how to watch on TV and live stream READ MORE :

"Would you sign him Geoff? Would you sign him?" Doyle asked, completely misjudging the mood. As the microphone was thrust in his face, Parling said: “That’s not on. That’s f***ing not on. Get off the pitch.”

He then gave Doyle a light push to the shoulder. The Irishman took the hint, quickly retreating to the sidelines, where Brian O'Driscoll was waiting as a pundit.

‌



"Oh sorry," Doyle said, taken aback. "OK. Geoff not happy about that. Apologies for language. Geoff understandably not happy about that."

The incident didn't go down well on social media, with some pointing the finger at Parling for his behaviour. "Should grow up", one person wrote under a clip of the incident on X.

View 2 Images The Leicester coach was furious at TNT Sports interrupting his warm-up (Image: TNT Sports)

‌



"Parling should not be swearing anywhere near TV cameras. Unacceptable on every level," a second wrote. Another added: "What an idiot ….wants to remember who funds top rugby."

TNT Sports pay Prem Rugby around £40million per year for exclusive coverage of the competition. And the confrontation is likely to have ruffled a few feathers behind the scenes, suggesting there is friction between the clubs and broadcaster.

However, some viewers took Parling's side. "To all those saying 'tv money' that doesn’t mean you get to kick balls into the players warming up before a game. They’ve bought the rights to show the games not the rights to the games themselves," one wrote. Another added: "Stupid and disrespectful place to be trying that."

Article continues below

The incident did not impact Leicester, who raced into a 22-0 lead inside the first 20 minutes. Will Wand and Gabriel Hamer-Webb both scored before Jamie Blamire touched down twice to put Gloucester out of the game.

The Tigers lead 29-5 at the break in front of a sizable crowd at Villa Park, the home of Premier League club Aston Villa. Gloucester hit back in the second half but were well beaten.

Sports.title: Inside Gabby Logan’s life after threat to quit TV and two heartbreaking deaths
Sports.content:
Inside Gabby Logan’s life after threat to quit TV and two heartbreaking deaths

Gabby Logan is a broadcasting legend with a career that's spanned across three decades

View 4 Images Gabby Logan, husband Kenny and children Lois and Reuben (Image: FRANK CORNFIELD)

Gabby Logan is scheduled to feature as a guest on Michael McIntyre's The Wheel on BBC One this weekend. Logan, 52, will be joined by Chris McCausland, McIntyre, Iain Stirling, Rachel Riley and others as they attempt to assist contestants in answering their way to a substantial prize on Saturday evening.

‌



She is, naturally, well accustomed to appearing on BBC programmes. With a portfolio spanning everything from Match of the Day to the Six Nations, Logan has cemented herself as one of the most recognisable and respected figures in sports broadcasting throughout her nearly three-decade-long television career.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



This has also seen her front major events such as the Olympics, the World Cup and the Euros. However, Logan's life beyond broadcasting has similarly attracted attention in recent years, with the presenter comfortable discussing her personal circumstances.

Here, Mirror Sport provides you with a comprehensive breakdown of everything we know about the veteran broadcaster, who joined the BBC back in 2007.

Family heartbreak

Logan was presenting Match of the Day live in January when she had to be substituted midway through the programme by Mark Chapman owing to a family emergency. It subsequently emerged that her dad, Terry Yorath, had passed away aged 75 following a short illness.

‌



Gabby Logan breaks down in tears over her father and heartbreaking phone call READ MORE :

Yorath was a Welsh football legend, celebrated for his successful playing and managerial career. He played for several clubs, including Leeds United, Tottenham Hotspur and Swansea City, while earning 59 caps as a midfielder for Wales.

The mother-of-two Logan was "shocked" by his death despite knowing he had a limited amount of time left. She had previously discussed the tragic loss of her younger brother Daniel back in 1992 and the profound impact it had on her family.

View 4 Images Terry Yorath with his daughter Gabby Logan (Image: Mike Egerton/EMPICS Sport/PA Wire)

‌



Daniel died at the age of 15 while playing football with their father. A post-mortem examination revealed that he had died from undiagnosed hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) - a congenital heart condition.

Speaking about that period on BBC Wales Live, she recalled: "He was playing football in the garden with my dad and he fell over. My dad thought he just was messing about and went over and rolled him over, and he basically died on the spot - and had no previous indications whatsoever that there were any health problems at all with him.

"It's like a sledgehammer coming down and sending everybody off in different directions, because it's such a catastrophic thing to happen, and no warning - and everybody responds differently."

‌



A statement released by Yorath's children following his death read: "To most he was a revered footballing hero, but to us he was Dad; a quiet, kind and gentle man. Our hearts are broken but we take comfort knowing that he will be reunited with our brother, Daniel."

Row with TV bosses

Before Logan's distinguished career with the BBC even kicked off, she was worried it had already ended when a boss who took a disliking to her removed her from covering a World Cup at ITV.

"I think I had a tough time in my early 30s in my career, and I can't believe that's nearly 20 years ago," Logan admitted on the White Wine Question Time podcast. "At the time, I thought it was going to be the end of my career.

‌



"I was departing ITV and given another opportunity by the BBC... because even in my early 30s, I felt I'd already had this really lengthy career and I'd done loads of fantastic work. I'd started off at Sky, moved to ITV and did loads of brilliant things.

View 4 Images Gabby Logan is a broadcasting icon (Image: Alex Livesey - Danehouse, Getty Images)

"But I kind of felt I was perhaps going to have to change direction and do something completely different because I had a boss that wasn't really into me and wanted to slightly demote me. He pulled me off the World Cup in 2006.

‌



"My kids were a year old and I was jetting off to Germany, leaving my little ones behind, heading to the World Cup. [But] in the middle of the World Cup, he sort of side-lined me, and I returned home with my tail between my legs.

"I was scheduled to cover many knockout games and he said [not to return] basically. Go home and don't come back because I was due to go home for a few days.

"In between the end of the group stages and the knockout stages, there's always a couple of days off and I was planning to go home because it was only Germany and the kids were a year old." Logan subsequently departed ITV for another role at the BBC, where she has remained ever since.

‌



Giant rugby star son

Logan's son, Reuben, is rapidly becoming a fully-fledged rugby star in his own right. The 20-year-old, who stands at an imposing 6ft 5in, has progressed from being a minor internet celebrity to turning heads in rugby circles lately.

He initially made headlines in 2024 when he was compared to Gary Barlow's towering son, Daniel, though Reuben is even taller.

View 4 Images Gabby Logan with her husband Kenny and their two children Reuben and Lois (Image: INSTAGRAM/GABBYLOGAN)

‌



The young Logan is now following in his dad Kenny's footsteps, switching his allegiance to represent Scotland despite previously playing for England. Reuben is a flanker for Sale Sharks and was loaned out to Championship side Caldy until the end of the 2025 season.

Adding to the family's remarkable sporting pedigree, Reuben also has a twin sister, Lois, who's making her mark in the equestrian world. She initially made waves in show-jumping but has since switched to racing after a stint at Glorious Goodwood.
